commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair job is needed because the PR head addresses the prior docs findings; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and CI gating.; Cleared: Cleared; the diff only changes Markdown docs and does not touch executable code, workflows, dependencies, package metadata, release scripts, or secrets. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates docs to compare OpenClaw code mode with Codex Code Mode, adds a Codex harness cross-link, and aligns related Codex stability wording across the Code mode and Tool Search pages.

PR surface: Docs +29. Total +29 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for docs-source consistency: current main has the older ambiguous/stale docs wording, and the PR head can be checked directly against OpenClaw source plus upstream Codex source. This is not a runtime reproduction because the patch is docs-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair job is needed because the PR head addresses the prior docs findings; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and CI gating.

target.title: docs: clarify OpenClaw vs Codex code mode
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #83390.
- Adds a side-by-side comparison table for OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode in `docs/reference/code-mode.md`.
- Adds a reverse link from `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md` so Codex harness readers can find the comparison anchor.
- Corrects Codex Code Mode wording from stale `exec.command` shell-command phrasing to upstream-backed raw JavaScript source through `exec`.
- Aligns **all** Codex stability wording across the touched docs surface with upstream Codex `Stage::UnderDevelopment` (ClawSweeper P2): the comparison table/prose, the `code-mode.md` Runtime status bullet, and the sibling `docs/tools/tool-search.md` no longer call Codex-native code mode / tool search / deferred dynamic tools "stable".
- No runtime, config, provider, or migration behavior changes.

## Scope note

This PR now also touches `docs/tools/tool-search.md` (sibling page, not in the original diff). ClawSweeper flagged that page plus the `code-mode.md` Runtime status bullet as carrying the same stale "stable Codex harness surface" claim this PR is meant to correct; leaving them would publish self-contradictory stability guidance. The added edits are the same one-invariant docs-accuracy fix (drop "stable", say upstream-under-development), kept minimal.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Readers were unclear how OpenClaw native code mode differs from Codex Code Mode (defaults, runtime owner, policy path, and `exec` contract). The docs also asserted in multiple places that Codex Code Mode / native tool search are "stable Codex harness surfaces", which contradicts upstream Codex feature metadata. This docs-only patch adds the comparison table and cross-link and corrects every stability claim on the touched surface, without changing runtime behavior.

**Real environment tested:**
OpenClaw repo checkout on Windows 10 + Node 22, patched branch `docs/issue-83390-code-mode-clarity` rebased onto current `origin/main`, head `dd3ab6cc4203`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
git diff --check
rg -ni "stable" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/tools/tool-search.md
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal capture from the patched branch showing docs validation passing and no remaining Codex stability claim:

```text
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (680 files, 7337ms).

$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4466
broken_links=0

$ git diff --check
(no output)

$ rg -ni "stable" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/tools/tool-search.md
docs/reference/code-mode.md:389:4. OpenClaw re-registers host callbacks by stable names.
docs/reference/code-mode.md:755:Catalog ids are stable within one run and deterministic across equivalent tool
docs/reference/code-mode.md:883:- register host callbacks by stable names
```

(The only remaining `stable` hits are about callback names and catalog ids — no Codex feature-stability claim remains; `tool-search.md` has none.)

Upstream source cross-checked (openai/codex `codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs`): `code_mode`, `code_mode_only`, and `deferred_executor` are all `stage: Stage::UnderDevelopment`; `search_tool` is `Stage::Removed`. None of the Codex-native code-mode/tool-search surfaces are stable.

**Observed result after fix:**
- MDX validation passes for the full docs tree (680 files).
- Internal link audit reports `broken_links=0`, including the cross-link to `#openclaw-code-mode-vs-codex-code-mode`.
- Every Codex stability reference on the touched pages now describes the surfaces as Codex-owned and upstream-under-development, giving one consistent stability story across `code-mode.md` and `tool-search.md`.

**What was not tested:**
Live Mintlify preview on docs.openclaw.ai; runtime Codex app-server or OpenClaw code-mode execution paths (docs-only change).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`
- `node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg -ni "stable" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/tools/tool-search.md`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? No
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: Docs wording only; incorrect comparison text could mislead readers.
Mitigation: Wording verified against current `src/agents/code-mode.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts`, and upstream Codex source (`codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs` stages, `codex-rs/core/src/tools/code_mode/execute_handler.rs` exec contract).

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review on head `dd3ab6cc4203`, then maintainer merge.
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient` and final maintainer review.
Bot comments addressed:
- P2 stability finding, round 2 (`docs/reference/code-mode.md:28-30`): the first fix corrected only the new comparison table/prose; ClawSweeper then found 4 remaining stale "stable Codex" claims. This round removes all of them — the `code-mode.md` Runtime status bullet (line 170) and the three `docs/tools/tool-search.md` references — so the docs no longer simultaneously call Codex code mode under development and stable.
- Source-aligned against openai/codex `features/src/lib.rs` (code_mode / code_mode_only / deferred_executor = `UnderDevelopment`, search_tool = `Removed`).
- Rebased onto current `origin/main`; re-ran docs MDX + link checks; `rg` confirms no stability claim remains on the touched pages.

Closes #83390.
target.author: pick-cat

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
